chapter_title.ar: بَابُ تَوْفِيرِ الشَّعْرِ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ الْحَجَّ وَ الْعُمْرَةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 73 – Letting grow the hair for the one who intends the Hajj and the Umra

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/234)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/234)
text: لَا يَأْخُذُ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا رَأَى هِلَالَ ذِي الْقَعْدَةِ وَ أَرَادَ الْخُرُوجَ مِنْ رَأْسِهِ وَ لَا مِنْ لِحْيَتِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from one of our companions, from Saeed Al A’araj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The man, when he sees the crescent of Zil Qadah, and he intends the going, should neither take (hair) from his head not from his beard’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Ali from certain persons of our people from Sa‘id al-‘Araj who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A man who wants to go for al-Hajj must not remove any hair from his head or beard in the month of Dhu al-Qa’dah.’”
